blocks.body: Passive Voice Basics is the introductory level for learning passive structures in English. Here you will learn how the passive is formed in Present Simple and Past Simple and when to use it.

{"level":"A2","intro":"In this topic you will learn the basics of the passive voice: the basic structure, how it is formed in Present Simple and Past Simple, and when to use the passive instead of the active.","rules":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","text":"Basic formula: be + Past Participle (V3):","example":"The letter is written."},{"label":"Present Simple","color":"blue","text":"Present tense: am/is/are + V3:","example":"English is spoken here."},{"label":"Past Simple","color":"rose","text":"Past tense: was/were + V3:","example":"The house was built in 1990."}]}

{"title":"1. What is the passive voice?","intro":"In the passive voice, the focus is on the action or its result rather than on the doer:","items":[{"label":"Active","title":"Active Voice","subtitle":"The subject performs the action: Tom writes letters."},{"label":"Passive","title":"Passive Voice","subtitle":"The subject receives the action: Letters are written by Tom."},{"label":"When?","title":"Usage","subtitle":"The doer is unknown, unimportant, or obvious."},{"label":"Agent","title":"Agent","subtitle":"by + doer (optional): by Tom."}]}

{"title":"2. Present Simple Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: am/is/are + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"English is spoken in many countries.","ua":"English is spoken in many countries."},{"en":"The rooms are cleaned every day.","ua":"The rooms are cleaned every day."},{"en":"I am paid monthly.","ua":"I am paid monthly."}]},{"label":"Negatives","color":"rose","description":"We add not after be: am/is/are + not + V3","examples":[{"en":"The door is not locked.","ua":"The door is not locked."},{"en":"These cars are not made in Japan.","ua":"These cars are not made in Japan."}]},{"label":"Questions","color":"blue","description":"Am/Is/Are comes first: Is/Are + S + V3?","examples":[{"en":"Is English spoken here?","ua":"Is English spoken here?"},{"en":"Are the letters sent daily?","ua":"Are the letters sent daily?"}]}]}

{"title":"3. Past Simple Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: was/were + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"The house was built in 1990.","ua":"The house was built in 1990."},{"en":"The thieves were caught yesterday.","ua":"The thieves were caught yesterday."},{"en":"I was invited to the party.","ua":"I was invited to the party."}]},{"label":"Negatives","color":"rose","description":"We add not after was/were: was/were + not + V3","examples":[{"en":"The email was not sent.","ua":"The email was not sent."},{"en":"We were not informed about it.","ua":"We were not informed about it."}]},{"label":"Questions","color":"blue","description":"Was/Were comes first: Was/Were + S + V3?","examples":[{"en":"Was the car repaired?","ua":"Was the car repaired?"},{"en":"Were the documents signed?","ua":"Were the documents signed?"}]}]}

{"title":"4. Active vs Passive","intro":"How to transform a sentence from the active voice into the passive:","rows":[{"en":"Active: Tom writes letters.","ua":"Tom writes letters.","note":"→ Passive: Letters are written by Tom."},{"en":"Active: She cleaned the room.","ua":"She cleaned the room.","note":"→ Passive: The room was cleaned by her."},{"en":"Active: They make cars in Germany.","ua":"They make cars in Germany.","note":"→ Passive: Cars are made in Germany."}],"warning":"📌 The object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive!"}

{"title":"5. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Omitting the verb be.","wrong":"The letter written yesterday.","right":"✅ The letter was written yesterday."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the wrong form of be.","wrong":"The letters was sent.","right":"✅ The letters were sent."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using V2 instead of V3.","wrong":"The house was builded.","right":"✅ The house was built."}]}

{"title":"6. Quick summary","items":["The passive voice focuses on the action or result, not the doer.","Basic structure: be + Past Participle (V3).","Present Simple Passive: am/is/are + V3.","Past Simple Passive: was/were + V3.","Negatives: be + not + V3.","Questions: Be + Subject + V3?","The agent is introduced with by, but it is often omitted."]}
